title: Joca Prepares for the Interview
description: Future tenses: WILL vs GOING TO. Joca has an interview at a US startup and the class predicts, plans, and promises.
summary_pt_br: WILL (decisões na hora, previsões) x GOING TO (planos já feitos, evidências visíveis). Joca se prepara para uma entrevista de trabalho remota numa startup dos EUA.

scenes.subtitle_en: Joca's shot at a remote job
scenes.subtitle_pt: A chance do Joca num trabalho remoto

scenes.text_en:
Joca stared at his calendar. Friday. 3 p.m. sharp.
'I'M GOING TO wear my blue shirt,' he said, 'and I'll sharpen my charm.
I'M GOING TO study their product. I'll know it by heart.
And if the wifi cuts out, I'LL use my phone. Smart.'
scenes.text_pt:
O Joca olhou o calendário. Sexta. 15h em ponto.
'VOU USAR minha camisa azul,' disse. 'E vou afiar meu tino.
VOU ESTUDAR o produto deles. Vou saber tudo.
Se o wifi cair, USO o celular. Esperto, meu rumo.'

scenes.title: WILL vs GOING TO — the core split
scenes.body_en: GOING TO = plans already made, decisions in place, predictions based on visible evidence. 'I'M GOING TO study at MIT' (already decided). 'Look at those clouds — it's GOING TO rain.' WILL = spontaneous decisions at the moment, predictions based on opinion, promises, offers. 'Ok, I'LL help.' 'I think it WILL rain tomorrow.'
scenes.body_pt: GOING TO = plano já feito, evidência visível. 'I'm going to study at MIT' (já decidi). WILL = decisão na hora, opinião, promessa, oferta. 'I'll help you' (acabou de decidir).

examples.en: A: Who's closing the door? B: I'll close it.
We're going to visit Orlando next July.
I think the Lakers will win.

exercise.question: The ceiling starts leaking. You grab a bucket. 'Hold on, I ___ get a bucket.' What fits?
exercise.explanation: Spontaneous reaction = WILL. You didn't plan it — you're deciding right now.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Decisão na hora = will. Sem plano prévio.

exercise.question: 'I bought the ticket last week. I ___ visit my brother in Orlando.' Which?
exercise.hint_pt_br: Plano firme (já comprou passagem) → going to.

exercise.question: Promise: 'Don't worry — I ___ call you as soon as I land.' (promise)
exercise.explanation: Promises use WILL. 'I will call you'.

turns.en: A little. But I've prepared. I think I'll do well.
turns.pt: Um pouco. Mas me preparei. Acho que vou bem.

turns.en: I'll send you a good-luck text at 2:55.
turns.pt: Vou te mandar uma mensagem de boa sorte às 14h55.

turns.en: Thanks. I'm going to need it.

exercise.explanation: Four contexts, two forms. Learn the trigger, not the verb.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro contextos, duas formas. Pense no gatilho, não no verbo.

exercise.question: Complete: "Sure. Let's ___ together."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "Sure. Let's practice together."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. ('Criativo e aprende rápido.')
options: You are creative and you learn quickly.
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "You are creative and you learn quickly."

scenes.title: Joca's interview prep
scenes.pt_summary: Preparação de entrevista — strengths, weaknesses, goals.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I have a big interview tomorrow. Can you help?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: You are creative and you learn quickly.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Why do I want this job?
turns.pt: Por que eu quero esse emprego?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Because you want to grow your career.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.pt: Qual minha meta de cinco anos?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: You want to become a team leader.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Thanks. I feel more confident now.

scenes.title: Listening story — Joca Prepares for the Interview
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Joca and a student. Future tenses: WILL vs GOING TO. Joca has an interview at a US startup and the class predicts, plans, and promises. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: I have a big interview tomorrow. Can you help?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: You are creative and you learn quickly.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Joca. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Joca Prepares for the Interview". Future tenses: WILL vs GOING TO. Joca has an interview at a US startup and the class predicts, plans, and promises. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Joca Prepares for the Interview". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Joca Prepares for the Interview'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Joca Prepares for the Interview".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: Perfect English Grammar
